zh.lifeSummary: 當Edmund de Bois於大約1243年出生在英國肯特時，他的父親William de Bois65歲，母親Maude Bonyngton33歲。他於大約1279年與Mary在英國結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世在英國英格蘭，埋葬在英國英格蘭肯特文加姆。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Edmund de Bois于大约1243年出生在英国肯特时，他的父亲William de Bois65岁，母亲Maude Bonyngton33岁。他于大约1279年与Mary在英国结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世在英国英格兰，埋葬在英国英格兰肯特文加姆。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Bois
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alcide, Antoine, Donat, Marcel, Ovide, Ovila, Raoul, Serge.

French: topographic name for someone living or working in a wood, from (Old) French bois ‘wood’ (from Late Latin boscum ‘bush, shrub, undergrowth’, of ancient Germanic origin), or a habitational name from (Le) Bois, the name of several places in various parts of France. This surname is also found in Haiti. Compare Debois and the most frequent French form Dubois . Compare also Boie , Boies , and Boyce .
English: variant of   Boyce   and   Boyes  .

variants.name: Dubois
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Normand, Jacques, Pierre, Fernand, Marcel, Emile, Lucien, Michel, Alain, Luc.

French, Walloon, and English (of French Huguenot origin): topographic name, with fused preposition and definite article du ‘from the’, for someone living or working in a wood (see Bois ), or a habitational name for someone from any of several places in various parts of France and in Belgium (Wallonia) named Le Bois. This is the most frequent surname in Wallonia. It is also found in the Flemish part of Belgium, the Netherlands, Germany, and Haiti. In both England and North America it is sometimes translated into English as Wood . Compare Deboe , Debois , Deboy , Duboise , and Dubose .

History: This surname is listed (in the form Du Bois) in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America. One of the several Huguenot ancestors bearing this name was Chrétien Du Bois from Flanders, whose son, Louis Du Bois (born 1626 in Lille, France, died 1696 in Kingston, NY), was among the Huguenots who founded the town of New Paltz, NY 1675–78.

variants.name: Boisvert
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Armand, Andre, Donat, Lucien, Jacques, Gaston, Normand, Pierre, Gilles, Herve, Adrien.
French:
topographic name for someone who lived in a dense forest or perhaps a copse of evergreens, from Old French bois ‘wood’ (see Bois ) + vert, verd ‘green’.

variants.name: Boisclair
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Normand, Marcel, Aldege, Alphonse, Arsene, Edmour, Emile, Leopaul, Prudent, Rejean, Remi, Yves.

French: topographic name from Old French bois ‘wood’ (see Bois ) + clair ‘clear, light’, or a habitational name from (Le) Bois Clair, the name of several places in various parts of France. This surname is virtually non-existing in France.

variants.name: Boies
meanings: Altered form of French   Bois  .

variants.name: Baskerville
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Boscherville in Eure, France, named with Old Anglo-Norman French boschet ‘copse, thicket’ (a diminutive of Bois ) + ville ‘settlement, town’.

variants.name: Debois
meanings: Altered form of French   Dubois  , a cognate of 1 above.
French: topographic name, with fused preposition de ‘from’, for someone living or working in a wood (see Bois ).

nameMeanings.name: Edmund
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + mund ‘protector’. It was borne by several early royal and saintly figures, including a 9th-century king of East Anglia killed by invading Danes, allegedly for his adherence to Christianity. In the 16th and 17th centuries, there was a good deal of interchange between Edmund and Edward.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Edmund de Bois was born about 1243, in Kent, England, his father, William de Bois, was 65 and his mother, Maude Bonyngton, was 33. He married Mary about 1279, in England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. He died in England, United Kingdom, and was buried in Wingham, Kent, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Edmund de Bois est né environ 1243, à Kent, Angleterre, son père, William de Bois, avait 65 ans et sa mère, Maude Bonyngton, avait 33 ans. Il a épousé Mary environ 1279, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, et a été enterré à Wingham, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb in England, United Kingdom und wurde in Wingham, Kent, England, United Kingdom begraben.

Lui sposato Mary circa 1279, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito, e fu sepolto a Wingham, Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Edmund de Boisが約1243年にイギリスケントで生まれた時，父William de Boisは65歳，母Maude Bonyngtonは33歳であった。約1279年にイギリスでMaryと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼はイギリスイギリスで死去し，イギリスイギリスケントウィンガムにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Edmund de Bois(이)가 대략 1243년 영국 켄트에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William de Bois(은)는 65세였고, 그의 어머니 Maude Bonyngton(은)는 33세였다. 그는 대략 1279년 영국에서 Mary(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 영국 잉글랜드에서 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 윙함에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Edmund de Bois nasceu aproximadamente 1243, em Kent, Inglaterra, seu pai, William de Bois, tinha 65 anos e sua mãe, Maude Bonyngton, tinha 33 anos. Ele casou-se com Mary aproximadamente 1279, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em Inglaterra, United Kingdom, e foi sepultado em Wingham, Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Edmund de Bois родился около 1243 г. в местности кэнт, Англия, его отцу по имени William de Bois было 65 лет, а матери, которую звали Maude Bonyngton, 33. Он женился на женщине по имени Mary около 1279 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер в местности Англия, Великобритания и был похоронен в местности вингхам, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания.

Se casó con Mary aproximadamente 1279, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en Inglaterra, Reino Unido, y fue sepultado en Wingham, Kent, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
